allies.rationale_text: Schema-acquisition supports perceptual-learning by turning repeated cue exposure into stable patterns that can be retrieved and updated later.
allies.source_quote: Its core definition and purpose involve the development of mental models or schemas that represent external reality.

allies.rationale_text: Cognitive-load-theory supports perceptual-learning by freeing working memory so meaningful cues can consolidate into usable patterns.
allies.source_quote: PL reduces cognitive load. By developing stable patterns and chunks of information, the working memory is freed up.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Perceptual-learning conflicts with confirmation-bias when prior pattern expectations suppress anomaly detection.
structured_tensions.source_quote: perceptual-learning vs confirmation-bias: perceptual-learning conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when prior pattern expectations suppress anomaly detection.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Perceptual-learning conflicts with status-quo-bias when practitioners stop updating their pattern libraries after initial mastery.
structured_tensions.source_quote: perceptual-learning vs status-quo-bias: perceptual-learning conflicts with status-quo-bias (status quo bias) when practitioners stop updating pattern libraries after initial mastery.

curation_notes.summary: The source is strongest on schema formation, load management, and an explicit tension block around entrenchment and missed anomalies.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader communication or empathy relation sprawl beyond the cleanest current-vocabulary learning links.
open_questions: Abstraction is source-adjacent in the communication material, but the first-order surface is cleaner around schema-acquisition and CLT.
